restaurant_name_zh: 玲珑 (上海外滩华尔道夫酒店店)
cuisine_type: Contemporary Chinese (modern 新中餐) — tasting menu
address: Waldorf Astoria Shanghai on the Bund (the 120-year-old heritage building, formerly the Shanghai Club / 上海总会, 1909)
head_chef: Jason Liu 刘禾森 (Taiwan-born; opened Ling Long Beijing 2019 — earned Beijing Michelin 1-star in 2022 as Young Chef Award winner; runs both Beijing and Shanghai venues)
restaurant_group: Restaurant Ling Long (Beijing + Shanghai, independent — distinct from hotel F&B operation)
signature_dishes: 3-year-old Tangshan black-footed chicken soup, dry-aged 3 months, served with fresh chicken and Yunnan wild matsutake
vibe_and_space: Inside the heritage Waldorf Astoria building on the Bund — the 1909 Shanghai Club building, hence the Chinese name 上海总会店. High-end hotel-restaurant setting with historic-building gravitas. Tasting menu format, no à-la-carte. Wine list is strong; Star Wine List–featured.
one_paragraph_recommendation: Jason Liu's Shanghai sister venue to his Beijing original (which opened 2019, Michelin-starred 2022). The Shanghai branch had its soft opening March 12 2023 and earned a Michelin star the same year — a same-year-as-opening star is rare. Promoted from Black Pearl 一钻 → 二钻 in 2026, joining 中国菜·头灶 in the Shanghai 升钻 cohort. The 8-course tasting at ¥1,680+ (wine pairing +¥980) is the centerpiece; the 3-year-old Tangshan chicken soup dry-aged 3 months is the canonical signature dish. Recommend for a special occasion when you want both fine-dining cooking AND historic-building setting (the 1909 Shanghai Club is itself a destination). Open question: how does the Shanghai Ling Long compare in cooking-quality terms to the Beijing original? TastyTrip's 'varied experience under 1 Michelin star' framing suggests the two venues diverge enough that they're worth treating as related-but-not-equivalent.
contradictions: The 'Beijing original vs Shanghai sister' comparison — TastyTrip headline explicitly frames the two venues as 'varied experience' despite both holding 1 star. Same chef, same brand, different teams on the ground — a potential template for tracking how chef-brand replication holds up across cities. Watch for sustained 公众号 long-form comparison reviews in 2026.
